130.source: Jake loved football more than anything. He hadn't eaten much since breakfast and his stomach growled. As the game continued, he couldn't help but lose focus. The hungry child dribbled all over the football pitch. He was looking to play the best pass so he would impress the coach, but he just couldn't concentrate over his rumbling stomach.

1342.source: John started a biking adventure last month. He rides past the local marketplace on his favorite track. Recently, people have noticed fewer stalls on his route. The market has been downhill lately, and this is really affecting John.

1410.target: toward a lower or inferior state
1410.source: Jack had always loved the thrill of mountain biking. Every weekend, he packed his gear and headed to the trails. He felt alive and unstoppable once he left the familiar comfort of home. He started going downhill after leaving home. He had overtaxed himself and felt winded; Jack felt stupid for not noticing the signs sooner.

1474.source: The kids decided to build a soapbox car. They worked hard all afternoon, excited to race it on the steep hill by their street. As they lined it up at the top, they laughed and cheered in anticipation. They didn't realize how fast it would all go downhill. Some of the nails were falling out already as they pushed it to the start; they weren't sure it would make it through the journey.

1600.target: the radioactive particles that settle to the ground after a nuclear explosion
1600.source: The fallout from the explosion contaminated nearby soil.

1601.target: The fallout from the explosion contaminated nearby soil.
1601.source: The fallout from the explosion contaminated nearby soil.

1602.target: the radioactive particles that settle to the ground after a nuclear explosion
1602.source: The experiment went horribly wrong. A huge plume rose into the sky. People watched from a distance, unsure of the outcome. The fallout was an impressive sight to behold. First, a bright light, then the shockwave, and outside the blast zone, those downwind had just minutes to escape before the radioactive cloud arrived.

1730.target: under the level of the ground
1730.source: Emily moved to the city last month. She quickly became fascinated by the intricate network beneath her new home. Every day, she explored different parts to understand its hidden mysteries. The underground system is one of the best. Every day, the Tube system sped people from home to work while above them, the city throbbed.

2818.target: deprive someone of benefits, as a penalty
2818.source: Sean had always dreamt of working by the sea. His job at the harbor kept him busy, overseeing shipments and coordinating arrivals. 2882.source: John was dining at his favorite local café, enjoying a quiet meal. He chatted with the friendly staff who knew him well. As he finished his dessert, he stood up to leave. 3010.source: James sat quietly at the bar, listening to the murmur of conversations around him. He had been coming here every evening since arriving in town, hoping to learn something valuable. The regulars were friendly enough and sometimes shared interesting pieces of information. There were some decent tips to be taken advantage of. It wasn't unusual to receive over 30% of the order value.

3074.target: a relatively small amount of money given for services rendered (as by a waiter)
3074.source: John had just moved to the city and was trying out the neighborhood's restaurants. That evening, he decided to visit a small Italian place around the corner. The food was delicious, and the service was friendly and attentive. After the meal, he shared some valued tips with the waiter. The look on the waiter's face was incredible gratitude when he received the money.

He hadn't seen a jack-in-the-box for years and thought everyone would enjoy it.

He hadn't seen a jack-in-the-box for years and thought everyone would enjoy it.

4162.source: Harry loved working in the garden, his hands always busy among the flowers. Over the years, he had developed a reputation for having an eye for the best blooms. However, his friends sometimes joked about how picky he could be with everything in life. Harry had a bad tendency to pick without discernment or discrimination. He just chooses what he wants and thinks about it later.

4354.target: pass from physical life and lose all bodily attributes and functions necessary to sustain life
4354.source: Lily was sitting by the pond, listening to the gentle rustling of the leaves. She had been visiting this spot every day, hoping to see some wildlife. A frog appeared on a rock, looking still and mysterious. The frog began to croak. It was a warning of what was to come next: a storm where the wildlife died.

4608.source: I prefer my steak cooked rare.

4609.target: I prefer my steak cooked rare.
4609.source: I prefer my steak cooked rare.

4610.target: (of meat) cooked a short time; still red inside
4610.source: James walked into the old butcher shop, eyeing the selection carefully. He remembered the legend of a steak so unique it could only be found once in a blue moon. The shopkeeper smiled, sensing James's curiosity as he pointed out a special cut. Well, this particular steak has to be rare. James understood then that this wasn't just about how the steak was cooked, but about the exceptional quality that made it a truly infrequent find.

4866.source: Lily had stayed up late the night before, going over her notes one last time. She knew how important this exam was for her future. Her alarm was set for early in the morning to give her plenty of time to prepare. By 6 a.m., she was already up and ready for the big exam. She started her day with eggs, bacon, and toast while drying her shirt by the heater.

4908.target: She was up on her studies.
4908.source: Lily had stayed up late the night before, going over her notes one last time. She knew how important this exam was for her future. Her alarm was set for early in the morning to give her plenty of time to prepare. By 6 a.m., she was already up and ready for the big exam. She was sure that she would pass her exams.

4930.source: The alarm rang, pulling him from his dreams. Rubbing his eyes, he glanced at the clock on the bedside table. The sun peeked through the curtains, hinting at a promising day ahead. He was up and eager to take on the day. With a happy strut, he entered the day with a smile, ready for action.

4992.target: a brief version of the essential meaning of something
4992.source: Make your point clearly and concisely.

4993.target: Make your point clearly and concisely.
4993.source: Make your point clearly and concisely.

4994.target: a brief version of the essential meaning of something
4994.source: Tom was deep in thought as he faced the cluttered shelf. On it, there lay a map rolled up beside an old hunting knife. He knew he had to make his decision now. He got to the point. Unfurling the map, he traced a finger along the coastline until he found what he was looking for. "There! That's where we'll go fishing." And with that, he pocketed the knife and ran out the door, down to the boatshed where everyone was waiting.

5122.source: Jack was nervous about the camping trip. He remembered the tales of wild animals in the woods. His friends insisted everyone bring only the essentials. He did bring a knife, but there was no point. He had forgotten the food, so what would he cut?
